If at any time the income of the fund is insufficient for the purposes of section 261, VANZ may, to the extent of the insufficiency, use the capital of the fund for those purposes.

history.history-note: Section 263: replaced, on 30 January 2021, by section 161 of the Trusts Act 2019 (2019 No 38).

prov.heading: How documents given or information notified

para.text: This section applies when this Act or regulations made under this Act—
require a document to be given to a person; or
require a person to be notified of any information, and references in this section to a document being given must be applied accordingly.

A document that this Act or regulations made under this Act require to be given to a person must be given by the method in subsection (3) that the person required to give the document considers is most likely to ensure that the document reaches the person.
The methods by which a document may be given are—
personally delivering it to the person; or
posting it to a usual address of the person; or
sending it to the person by fax or some other electronic means; or
providing it to the person in a manner approved by the person.
A document personally delivered under subsection (3)﻿(a) must, if delivered to a corporate body, be delivered to the corporate body's offices during working hours.

A document posted under subsection (3)﻿(b) is deemed to have been delivered to the person at the time it would have been delivered in the ordinary course of post. For the purposes of proving delivery,—
it is sufficient to prove that the document was properly addressed; and
the document is presumed, in the absence of proof to the contrary, to have been posted on the day on which it was dated.

A document sent under subsection (3)﻿(c) is deemed, in the absence of proof to the contrary, to be delivered on the day after the day on which it is sent, and it is sufficient proof of sending that a correct machine-generated acknowledgement of receipt exists.
This section is subject to any provision of this Act or regulations made under this Act that specifically provides how a document is to be given.

prov.heading: Regulations

para.text: The Governor-General may, by Order in Council, make regulations for the purposes of 1 or more of the following provisions:
section 7 (definitions of accepted late-onset condition and treatment provider):
section 20 (specifying which organs are paired organs):
section 21 (specifying conditions linked to specific exposure or specific events):
section 22 (application of statements of principles under Australian legislation):
section 23 (new statements of principles and revocation of statements of principles):
sections 30 and 31 (indexation of certain entitlements):
section 46 (travel costs associated with medical treatment):
section 56 (rates of disablement pension):
section 66 (rate of surviving spouse or partner pension):
section 72 (rate of children's pension):
section 76 (rate of dependant's pension):
section 81 (rate of children's bursary):
section 88 (transport costs associated with treatment):
section 90 (vocational services and assistance for spouse or partner of veteran):
section 96 (election to receive lump sum payment):
section 98 (lump sum payments):
section 109 (cost of ancillary services related to treatment):
section 140 (limits on scope of independence programme):
section 144 (veterans' independence programme):
section 145 (aids and appliances):
section 148 (motor vehicle grants):
section 150 (asset assessment):
section 151 (retirement lump sum):
section 153 (funeral expenses if death due to qualifying service and maximum amount for transferring body):
section 154 (funeral expenses if death not due to qualifying service):
section 156 (provision of plaques and headstones):
section 157 (provision of financial advice):
section 159 (forms of accommodation linked to meaning of living alone):
section 169 (rates of veteran's pension):
section 171 (abatement of veteran’s pension):
section 174 (lump sum payment on death):
section 208 (payments to persons other than claimants):
section 212 (fees and allowances of assessors):
section 216 (procedure for reviews):
section 221 (conduct of medical assessments):
section 229 (procedure for appeals):
section 235 (reimbursement of costs and travelling expenses of appellant, witnesses, and other participants):
section 240 (procedure of appeal board):
section 247 (procedure of advisory board):
section 254 (procedure of advisory panel):
Schedule 1 (transitional, savings, and related provisions):
Schedule 2 (entitlements).
The Governor-General may, by Order in Council, make regulations for any other matters contemplated by this Act, necessary for its administration, or necessary for giving it full effect.
Regulations under this section are secondary legislation (see Part 3 of the Legislation Act 2019 for publication requirements).

legtable.summary: The following table is small in size and has 3 columns that are grouped under the heading Legislation Act 2019 requirements for secondary legislation made under this section.

entry.emphasis: Legislation Act 2019 requirements for secondary legislation made under this section

row.entry: PCO must publish it on the legislation website and notify it in the Gazette
The Minister must present it to the House of Representatives
It may be disallowed by the House of Representatives

entry.emphasis: This note is not part of the Act.

history.history-note: Section 265(1)﻿(27A): inserted, on 9 November 2020, by section 33 of the New Zealand Superannuation and Veteran’s Pension Legislation Amendment Act 2020 (2020 No 36).
Section 265(1)﻿(29): repealed, on 1 October 2020, by section 31 of the Veterans’ Support Amendment Act 2020 (2020 No 54).
Section 265(1)﻿(29A): inserted, on 1 April 2020, by section 20 of the Social Assistance Legislation (Budget 2019 Welfare Package) Amendment Act 2019 (2019 No 23).
Section 265(1)﻿(40): amended, on 21 January 2019, by section 12 of the Social Assistance (Residency Qualification) Legislation Act 2018 (2018 No 45).
Section 265(3): inserted, on 28 October 2021, by section 3 of the Secondary Legislation Act 2021 (2021 No 7).

prov.heading: Treatment cards

para.text: The Governor-General may, by Order in Council, make regulations providing for treatment cards for veterans.

The purpose of a treatment card is to enable a veteran to give treatment providers access to information held by VANZ about the service-related injuries, illnesses, or conditions the veteran has and for which VANZ will pay or contribute towards the cost of treatment.
Without limiting subsection (1), regulations made under that subsection may—
specify—
the information to be included on a treatment card:
the obligations of a holder of a treatment card:
the conditions under which a treatment card may be used:
what uses of a treatment card are not authorised; and
prescribe when a treatment card is to be recalled and when a treatment card may be cancelled; and
prescribe when the holder of a treatment card may be exempted from any conditions under which the card may be used.
Regulations under this section are secondary legislation (see Part 3 of the Legislation Act 2019 for publication requirements).

entry.emphasis: This note is not part of the Act.

history.history-note: Section 266(4): inserted, on 28 October 2021, by section 3 of the Secondary Legislation Act 2021 (2021 No 7).

prov.heading: Regulations relating to treatment and rehabilitation

para.text: For the purposes of Part 4 and Schedule 2, the Governor-General may, on the recommendation of the Minister, by Order in Council, make regulations—
prescribing the costs that VANZ is liable to pay for the entitlement of treatment and rehabilitation:
prescribing the circumstances in which, and the method by which, VANZ must make any payment for treatment and rehabilitation:
prescribing the circumstances in which, and the method by which, VANZ may make arrangements, and make contributions, for treatment and rehabilitation:
prescribing the persons to whom those payments may be made.

The Minister must not make any recommendation under subsection (1) without first receiving a recommendation from VANZ and consulting the persons or organisations the Minister considers appropriate, having regard to the subject matter of the proposed regulations.
Without limiting the matters that may be prescribed by regulations, regulations made under subsection (1) may—
prescribe—
a percentage, or different percentages, of the total costs to be paid by VANZ; or
a specified amount, or specified amounts, that VANZ is liable to pay in respect of those costs in specified circumstances:
provide that VANZ is liable to make payments in respect of costs only to the extent that costs exceed amounts specified in the regulations:
provide that VANZ is liable to make payments in respect of treatment and rehabilitation only if a person who is a member of a class prescribed in the regulations—
provides treatment and rehabilitation; or
refers a claimant to treatment and rehabilitation; or
directly supervises the provision of treatment and rehabilitation:
provide that VANZ—
must not pay any of the cost of specified treatment and rehabilitation; or
is liable to pay some or all of such costs only if conditions specified in the regulations or by VANZ are met:
prescribe—
an amount that the veteran or claimant must or must not pay for treatment and rehabilitation that is treatment; or
a percentage that the veteran or claimant must or must not pay of the total amount payable for treatment.
Without limiting the matters that may be prescribed by regulations, regulations made under subsection (1) may contain different provisions in respect of—
payments relating to work-related injury or illness and payments relating to other injury or illness:
payments relating to claimants based on either or both of the following:
the need of a veteran or claimant as assessed by VANZ:
assessments conducted by or on behalf of any other relevant government agency:
payments to persons not resident in New Zealand at the time of receiving the payments.
Regulations under this section are secondary legislation (see Part 3 of the Legislation Act 2019 for publication requirements).

history.history-note: Section 267(5): inserted, on 28 October 2021, by section 3 of the Secondary Legislation Act 2021 (2021 No 7).

prov.heading: Regulations relating to ancillary services for rehabilitation

para.text: For the purposes of clause 6 of Schedule 2, the Governor-General may, on the recommendation of the Minister, by Order in Council, make regulations—
prescribing the costs that VANZ is liable to pay or contribute to paying in respect of 1 or more ancillary services that facilitate rehabilitation provided by VANZ in New Zealand:
prescribing the circumstances in which, and the method by which, VANZ is liable for any payment for 1 or more services ancillary to rehabilitation:
prescribing the circumstances in which, and the method by which, VANZ may make arrangements, and make contributions, for 1 or more services ancillary to rehabilitation:
prescribing the conditions that must be met, and the matters that must be taken into account:
prescribing the persons to whom those payments may be made.

The Minister must not make any recommendation under subsection (1) without first consulting the persons or organisations the Minister considers appropriate, having regard to the subject matter of the proposed regulations.
Without limiting the matters that may be prescribed by regulations, regulations made under subsection (1) may—
prescribe the circumstances in which VANZ is liable to pay or contribute to the costs of 1 or more ancillary services that facilitate rehabilitation, examples of which are that the service—
is necessary and appropriate:
is or will be provided by a person who is an appropriate provider of the particular ancillary service:
has been or will be provided only on the number of occasions necessary for that purpose:
has been agreed in an individual rehabilitation plan, if a plan has been agreed:
is provided after VANZ has agreed to the rehabilitation and after VANZ has agreed to the ancillary service in respect of the rehabilitation:
prescribe the matters that must be taken into account when VANZ is deciding whether and to what extent ancillary services that facilitate rehabilitation should be provided, including such matters as—
the nature and severity of the injury:
the rehabilitation outcome that will be achieved:
the claimant's assessed need for the ancillary services:
the other options available to meet the need:
the cost of the service and of the other options, compared with the benefit that the claimant is likely to receive from the ancillary service:
the responsibilities of the person in relation to his or her rehabilitation:
the geographic location in which the person lives:
provide that VANZ is to make payments only if specified service thresholds have been met or exceeded.
Regulations under this section are secondary legislation (see Part 3 of the Legislation Act 2019 for publication requirements).

history.history-note: Section 268(4): inserted, on 28 October 2021, by section 3 of the Secondary Legislation Act 2021 (2021 No 7).

prov.heading: Regulations relating to when conditions stabilise and to assessing whole-person impairment

para.text: For the purposes of Part 3 of Schedule 2, the Governor-General may, on the recommendation of the Minister, by Order in Council, make regulations specifying when a claimant's condition is to be regarded as stabilised for the purposes of this Act.
The Governor-General may, on the recommendation of the Minister, by Order in Council, make regulations for the purposes of assessing whole-person impairment, including regulations that—
refer to, or incorporate by reference, in whole or in part, the American Medical Association Guides to the Evaluation of Permanent Impairment:
refer to, or incorporate by reference, guides, frameworks, or other standards:
prescribe an assessment tool that in itself may refer to, or incorporate by reference, in whole or in part, any thing referred to in paragraph (a) or (b):
do any combination of the things referred to in paragraph (a), (b), or (c):
amend the lump sum compensation amounts or independence allowance payable under clause 46 of Schedule 2:

prescribe calculations and rules for determining the combined effects of injury or illness on a claimant who has suffered more than 1 injury or illness, for the purposes of clause 48(5) of Schedule 2:
prescribe calculations and rules for adjusting the whole-person impairment score of a claimant to take into account the effect of injuries suffered before 1 April 2002:

prescribe the scale of lump sum compensation amounts or independence allowance payable in respect of different degrees of whole-person impairment under clauses 46 and 49 of Schedule 2 so that the amount increases exponentially or otherwise as the degree of impairment increases:
prescribe such other matters as may be desirable to enable the assessment, calculation, and payment of lump sums or independence allowance.

The Minister must not make any recommendation under subsection (1) or (2) without first consulting the persons or organisations that the Minister considers appropriate, having regard to the subject matter of the proposed regulations.
Any material incorporated by reference under subsection (2)﻿(a) must be incorporated in accordance with, and subject to, sections 63 to 66 and Schedule 2 of the Legislation Act 2019.
Regulations under this section are secondary legislation (see Part 3 of the Legislation Act 2019 for publication requirements).

history.history-note: Section 269(4): amended, on 28 October 2021, by section 3 of the Secondary Legislation Act 2021 (2021 No 7).
Section 269(5): inserted, on 28 October 2021, by section 3 of the Secondary Legislation Act 2021 (2021 No 7).

prov.heading: Offence to mislead VANZ

para.text: A person commits an offence if the person, for the purpose described in subsection (2) or with the result described in subsection (3),—
makes any statement knowing it to be false in any material particular; or
wilfully does or says anything, or omits to do or say anything, for the purpose of misleading or attempting to mislead VANZ or any other person concerned in the administration of this Act.
The purpose is—
for the person to receive or continue to receive any payment or entitlement; or
for another person to receive or continue to receive any payment or entitlement.
The result is—
that the person receives or continues to receive any payment or entitlement, whether or not entitled to it under this Act; or
that another person receives or continues to receive any payment or entitlement, whether or not entitled to it under this Act.
A person who commits an offence against this section is liable on conviction—
to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 months; or
to a fine not exceeding $5,000.

prov.heading: Offence not to provide earnings information to VANZ

para.text: This section applies to a person who is receiving any of the following entitlements:
weekly compensation:
weekly income compensation:
a veteran's pension under—
section 161 if the pension is subject to abatement under section 171(1); or
section 164:
a dependant's pension.
A person to whom this section applies commits an offence if,—
after he or she began receiving any payment for the entitlements, his or her earnings have increased in such a way as to reduce the entitlement payable to him or her; and
he or she fails to advise VANZ of the matters in paragraph (a) as soon as practicable.
A person who commits an offence against this section is liable on conviction to a fine not exceeding $5,000.

history.history-note: Section 271(1)﻿(a): repealed, on 24 October 2019, by section 175 of the Statutes Amendment Act 2019 (2019 No 56).

prov.heading: Offence not to provide requested information to VANZ

para.text: This section applies to—
an employer or a former employer of a claimant:
a person who has provided any rehabilitation to a claimant:
a person who has sought or received any payment in respect of a claimant.

A person to whom this section applies must provide VANZ with any information or statement, when requested to do so by VANZ, for the purposes of facilitating decisions about cover and the timely and appropriate provision of entitlements, or detecting fraud.
A person to whom this section applies commits an offence if the person refuses or fails to supply, without reasonable excuse, the information or statement requested under subsection (2).
This section does not apply unless the claimant has authorised the request being made and the person to whom the request is made has notice of that authorisation.
A person who commits an offence against this section is liable on conviction to a fine not exceeding $2,000.

prov.heading: Offence to demand or accept assignment or charge of entitlement

para.text: A person must not, in relation to any entitlement under this Act, demand or accept any certificate, acknowledgment, or undertaking that would constitute a legal or an equitable assignment of or a charge upon the entitlement (if the entitlement were capable of being legally assigned or charged).
A person who contravenes subsection (1) commits an offence and is liable on conviction to a fine not exceeding $500.

prov.heading: Time for filing charge

para.text: Despite anything to the contrary in section 25 of the Criminal Procedure Act 2011, the limitation period in respect of an offence against this Act, or any regulations made under it, ends on the date that is 12 months after the date on which the facts alleged in the charging document are brought to the knowledge of the person by whom the proceedings are instituted.

prov.heading: General fine for offences

para.text: A person who commits an offence against this Act or any regulations made under it for which no fine is provided in this Act or its regulations, otherwise than in this section, is liable on conviction to a fine not exceeding $500.

subpart.heading: Repeals, transitional provisions, and consequential amendments

prov.heading: Repeals

para.text: The War Pensions Act 1954 (1954 No 54) and the Veterans' Affairs Act 1999 (1999 No 76) are repealed.

prov.heading: Application, savings, and transitional provisions

history.history-note: Section 277: repealed, on 21 January 2019, by section 13 of the Social Assistance (Residency Qualification) Legislation Act 2018 (2018 No 45).

prov.heading: Consequential amendments

para.text: Amend the enactments specified in Schedule 4 as set out in that schedule.

citation.leg-title: Housing Restructuring and Tenancy Matters Act 1992
Social Welfare (Reciprocity Agreements, and New Zealand Artificial Limb Service) Act 1990
Income Tax (Social Assistance Suspensory Loans) Order 1995
Social Security (Effect on Benefit of Warrant to Arrest—Excluded Beneficiaries) Regulations 2013
Social Security (Period of Income Assessment) Regulations 1996
Social Welfare (Reciprocity with Jersey and Guernsey) Order 1995
Social Welfare (Reciprocity with the Hellenic Republic) Order 1993
Social Welfare (Reciprocity with the United Kingdom) Order 1990

prov.heading: Veteran officially reported as missing

para.text: This section applies in relation to a veteran who is officially reported as missing while on qualifying operational service.

No pensions, benefits, or entitlements payable if the veteran had died while on qualifying operational service are payable until the veteran is officially reported as having died while on qualifying operational service.

If the veteran is officially reported as having died while on qualifying operational service and is subsequently found to be alive, then all pensions, benefits, and entitlements are cancelled on and from the date on which the veteran is found to be alive.

prov.heading: Arrangements with other countries

para.text: The Minister may enter into arrangements with the appropriate authority in any other country providing that the appropriate department of State of that country is to act in that country as agent for the Defence Force for any purpose under this Act, and that the Defence Force is to act in New Zealand as agent for that department of State for any purpose under any analogous law of that country.

There must be paid out of a Crown Bank Account, from money appropriated by Parliament for the purpose, all money required to be expended by the Minister or the Defence Force for the purpose of giving effect to any arrangement entered into under this section.

Without limiting the power to make regulations conferred by section 265, regulations may be made under that section to give full effect to the provisions of this section and to any arrangement entered into by the Minister under this section.

Any regulations made in accordance with subsection (3) may, so far as they relate to any particular arrangement, be made to come into force on the date on which the arrangement was entered into, even if that date is before the date of the making of the regulations.

prov.heading: When the Crown not liable to pay compensation or damages for death or disablement of veterans

para.text: No proceedings for compensation or damages lie against the Crown or any officer of the Crown in respect of the death or disablement of a veteran if—
any entitlement has at any time been provided or is being provided under this Act or the War Pensions Act 1954 in respect of the death or disablement; or

VANZ has determined that, for the purposes of this Act, the death or disablement is, or is to be treated as being, caused by, contributed to by, or aggravated by the service of the veteran with the armed forces, or that the condition that resulted in the death or disablement was aggravated by his or her service with the armed forces.

However, subsection (1) does not exempt an officer of the Crown from liability for any act or omission in any case where the court is satisfied that the act or omission was not connected with the execution of his or her duties as an officer of the Crown.
Any determination of VANZ under subsection (1) may be made by VANZ whether or not a claim for an entitlement has been made under this Act.

A certificate by the Chief of Defence Force to the effect that VANZ has determined that, for the purposes of this Act, the death or disablement of a veteran is, or is to be treated as being, caused by, contributed to by, or aggravated by his or her service with the armed forces, or that the condition that resulted in the death or disablement of the veteran was aggravated by his or her service with the armed forces, is for the purposes of this section conclusive as to the fact that it certifies.

prov.heading: Review of operation of Act

para.text: As soon as practicable after the second anniversary of the commencement of Part 3 (Scheme One), the Chief of Defence Force must—
review the operation of this Act; and
consider whether any amendments to this Act are necessary or desirable; and
report his or her findings to the Minister.
As soon as practicable after receiving the report, the Minister must present a copy of the report to the House of Representatives.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 heading: replaced, on 21 January 2019, by section 14(1) of the Social Assistance (Residency Qualification) Legislation Act 2018 (2018 No 45).
Schedule 1 heading: amended, on 21 January 2019, by section 14(2) of the Social Assistance (Residency Qualification) Legislation Act 2018 (2018 No 45).
Schedule 1 heading: amended, on 21 January 2019, by section 14(3) of the Social Assistance (Residency Qualification) Legislation Act 2018 (2018 No 45).

part.heading: Provisions relating to this Act as enacted

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 1 heading: replaced, on 21 January 2019, by section 14(4) of the Social Assistance (Residency Qualification) Legislation Act 2018 (2018 No 45).

part.crosshead: Savings relating to this Act as enacted

history.history-note: Heading: inserted, on 21 January 2019, by section 14(4) of the Social Assistance (Residency Qualification) Legislation Act 2018 (2018 No 45).

prov.heading: Allowances for services of attendant in certain cases

para.text: Section 29 of the War Pensions Act 1954 continues in force, as if this Act had not been passed, but only for the persons who (immediately before the commencement of this Act) were receiving allowances under that section.

prov.heading: Additional payments for decorations awarded

para.text: Section 29A of the War Pensions Act 1954 and Part 5A of the War Pensions Regulations 1956 continue in force as if this Act had not been passed, but only for the persons who (immediately before the commencement of this Act) were receiving or entitled to receive an annuity or allowance in addition to his or her pension.

However, the rates of annuities or allowances payable under subclause (1) must be adjusted in accordance with section 31(1)﻿(la) of this Act (and may not be prescribed under section 29A(3) of the War Pensions Act 1954).
This clause applies subject to Part 2.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 2(1A): inserted, on 24 October 2019, by section 176 of the Statutes Amendment Act 2019 (2019 No 56).

prov.heading: Continuation of allowances, grants, concessions, and loans

para.text: Any other pension allowance, grant, concession, or loan granted to a veteran or surviving spouse of a veteran under the War Pensions Act 1954 and the War Pensions Regulations 1956 and current immediately before the commencement of this Act continues as if this Act had not been passed.

prov.heading: Reviews and appeals

para.text: Any review or appeal under the War Pensions Act 1954 instituted but not concluded at the commencement of this Act may be continued and concluded as if this Act had not been passed.

prov.heading: Rights of review and appeal

para.text: Any right of review or appeal under the War Pensions Act 1954 that existed but which had not been exercised at the commencement of this Act may be exercised and the review or appeal continued and concluded as if this Act had not been passed.

prov.heading: War disablement pension

para.text: A veteran who is receiving a war disablement pension under Part 2 of the War Pensions Act 1954 immediately before the commencement of Part 3 of this Act is entitled to continue receiving the pension as if it were payable under Part 3 of this Act.

A veteran to whom subclause (1) applies may apply for a disablement pension under Part 3 of this Act and is entitled to receive a disablement pension in respect of any injury or illness for which a war disablement pension was granted to the veteran under Part 2 of the War Pensions Act 1954.

However, the rate of payment to a veteran under subclause (1) or (2) must not be less than the rate of payment that the veteran was entitled to receive immediately before the commencement of Part 3 of this Act.

For the purposes of this clause, the application for, and grant of, a disablement pension is to be treated as an application for, and grant of, a temporary disablement pension if, before the commencement of Part 3 of this Act, the veteran was receiving in relation to the same disablement both a permanent and temporary disablement pension.
VANZ must assess, in accordance with regulations made under section 265, the degree of whole-person impairment of a veteran who applies under subclause (2).

However, to avoid doubt, a veteran retains his or her entitlement to receive a disablement pension under Part 3 even though an assessment under subclause (5) indicates that the veteran's degree of whole-person impairment would not make the veteran eligible for a disablement pension under Part 3.
Subclause (2) does not preclude a rate of payment being adjusted if the rate of payment a veteran was receiving immediately before the commencement of Part 3 of this Act—
was not a rate of payment to which the veteran was entitled under the War Pensions Act 1954; or
is no longer a rate of payment to which the veteran is entitled under this Act.

part.crosshead: Transitional provisions relating to this Act as enacted

history.history-note: Heading: inserted, on 21 January 2019, by section 14(5) of the Social Assistance (Residency Qualification) Legislation Act 2018 (2018 No 45).

prov.heading: Terminal conditions

para.text: This clause applies to a veteran who, under clause 6, is entitled to continue to receive a war disablement pension as if it were payable under Part 3 of this Act.

The veteran may make an election under section 53 or make an application under section 54 as if he or she were receiving a disablement pension under Part 3 of this Act, and those sections apply accordingly.

prov.heading: Surviving spouse pension

para.text: A spouse or partner of a veteran who is receiving a surviving spouse pension under Part 2 of the War Pensions Act 1954, immediately before the commencement of Part 3 of this Act is entitled to continue receiving the pension as if it were payable under Part 3 of this Act.

The rate of payment to a surviving spouse or partner under subclause (1) must not be less than the rate of payment that the surviving spouse or partner was entitled to receive immediately before the commencement of Part 3 of this Act.
Subclause (2) does not preclude a rate of payment being adjusted if the rate of payment a surviving spouse or partner was receiving immediately before the commencement of Part 3 of this Act—
was not a rate of payment to which the surviving spouse or partner was entitled under the War Pensions Act 1954; or
is no longer a rate of payment to which the surviving spouse or partner is entitled under this Act.
For the purposes of this clause, the surviving spouse pension includes any parent's allowance paid under section 32(2) of the War Pensions Act 1954.

prov.heading: Children's pension

para.text: A child of a veteran who is receiving a children's pension under Part 2 of the War Pensions Act 1954, immediately before the commencement of Part 3 of this Act is entitled to continue receiving the pension as if it were payable under Part 3 of this Act.
The rate of payment to a child under subclause (1) must not be less than the rate of payment that the child was entitled to receive immediately before the commencement of Part 3 of this Act.
Subclause (2) does not preclude a rate of payment being adjusted if the rate of payment a child was receiving immediately before the commencement of Part 3 of this Act—
was not a rate of payment to which the child was entitled under the War Pensions Act 1954; or
is no longer a rate of payment to which the child is entitled under this Act.

prov.heading: Dependants

para.text: A dependant of a veteran who is receiving a dependant's pension under Part 2 of the War Pensions Act 1954, immediately before the commencement of Part 3 of this Act is entitled to continue receiving the pension as if it were payable under Part 3 of this Act.
The rate of payment to a dependant under subclause (1) must not be less than the rate of payment that the dependant was entitled to receive immediately before the commencement of Part 3 of this Act.
Subclause (2) does not preclude a rate of payment being adjusted if the rate of payment a dependant was receiving immediately before the commencement of Part 3 of this Act—
was not a rate of payment to which the dependant was entitled under the War Pensions Act 1954; or
is no longer a rate of payment to which the dependant is entitled under this Act.

prov.heading: War bursaries

para.text: Every child who, immediately before the commencement of Part 3 of this Act, is entitled to a war bursary under the War Pensions Act 1954 retains that entitlement.
For the purposes of subclause (1), the War Pensions Act 1954 continues to apply as if this Act had not been passed.

prov.heading: Certain allowances continued for specified period

para.text: This clause applies in relation to claims made and accepted under section 47 in the specified period.
During that period, the allowances specified in subclause (3) are payable as if the War Pensions Act 1954 had not been repealed.
The allowances are the following allowances payable under the War Pensions Act 1954:
attendant allowance:
clothing allowance:
travel allowance:
recreational travel concession.
A veteran becomes entitled to an allowance under this clause when—
the veteran has completed and provided to VANZ an application form provided by VANZ; and
the veteran has supplied any supporting evidence or information required by VANZ and in the form required by VANZ; and
VANZ has accepted the application.
If an application is accepted by VANZ, the veteran's entitlement to the allowance is to be treated as beginning on the day on which VANZ received the application.
In this clause, specified period means the period—
beginning on the commencement of Part 3 (Scheme One); and
ending on the close of the day before the commencement of Part 4 (Scheme Two).

prov.heading: Entitlement to equivalent allowances under the veterans' independence programme on and from commencement of Part 4 (Scheme Two)

para.text: This clause applies to the allowances and concession referred to in clause 12(3) that are payable under clause 1, 3, or 12(2).

On and from the commencement of Part 4 (Scheme Two), the veteran is entitled to receive, under the veterans' independence programme under subpart 4 of Part 5, support and services that include allowances that are not less than the allowances that were payable under clause 12.

prov.heading: Initial rates of veteran's pension

para.text: The rates of veteran's pension payable under Part 6 in the initial period must be equal to the corresponding rates in Schedule 11 as follows:
the single living alone rate payable in the initial period is the rate in clause 1(a) of that schedule:
the single sharing accommodation rate payable in the initial period is the rate in clause 1(b) of that schedule:
the relationship rate payable in the initial period is the rate in clause 1(c) of that schedule:
the relationship (partner not receiving superannuation or pension) rate payable in the initial period is the rate in clause 2(b) of that schedule:
the relationship (partner not receiving superannuation or pension) legacy rate payable in the initial period is the rate in clause 2(a) of that schedule.
In this clause,—
initial period means the period beginning with the commencement of Part 6 and ending at the end of the day that is the first occurrence of 31 March following that commencement
Schedule 11 means Schedule 11 of the War Pensions Act 1954, as it was immediately before the commencement of Part 6 of this Act.

prov.heading: Continuation of veteran’s pension

para.text: A person who is receiving a veteran’s pension under the War Pensions Act 1954 immediately before the commencement of Part 6 of this Act is entitled to continue receiving a veteran’s pension under Part 6 of this Act.

part.heading: Provisions relating to Social Assistance (Residency Qualification) Legislation Act 2018

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 2: inserted, on 21 January 2019, by section 14(6) of the Social Assistance (Residency Qualification) Legislation Act 2018 (2018 No 45).

prov.heading: Interpretation

para.text: In this Part,—
2001 Act means the New Zealand Superannuation and Retirement Income Act 2001
2018 Act means the Social Assistance (Residency Qualification) Legislation Act 2018.

prov.heading: Application of section 8(3) of the New Zealand Superannuation and Retirement Income Act 2001

para.text: This clause applies to a person who, on or after the date on which the 2018 Act comes into force, applies for a veteran’s pension.

In determining for the purposes of section 161(1)﻿(b) or (2)﻿(a), 162(1)﻿(b) or (2)﻿(a), or 163(1)﻿(a) or (2)﻿(a) of this Act whether the person is eligible to receive New Zealand superannuation and, specifically whether he or she meets the residential qualification under section 8(3) of the 2001 Act, the person’s residence and presence in any of the countries or the territory before the 2018 Act came into force must be taken into account.

In determining for the purposes of section 163(1)﻿(c) of this Act whether a veteran, had he or she not died, would have been eligible to receive New Zealand superannuation and, specifically whether the veteran would have met the residential qualification under section 8(3) of the 2001 Act, the veteran’s residence and presence in any of the countries or the territory before the 2018 Act came into force must be taken into account.

In determining for the purposes of section 191(4)﻿(c) whether a person has met the residential qualification for New Zealand superannuation under section 8(3) of the 2001 Act, the person’s residence and presence in any of the countries or the territory before the 2018 Act came into force must be taken into account.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 17 heading: amended, on 16 November 2021, by section 13(1) of the New Zealand Superannuation and Retirement Income (Fair Residency) Amendment Act 2021 (2021 No 46).
Schedule 1 clause 17(2): amended, on 16 November 2021, by section 13(2) of the New Zealand Superannuation and Retirement Income (Fair Residency) Amendment Act 2021 (2021 No 46).
Schedule 1 clause 17(3): amended, on 16 November 2021, by section 13(2) of the New Zealand Superannuation and Retirement Income (Fair Residency) Amendment Act 2021 (2021 No 46).
Schedule 1 clause 17(4): amended, on 16 November 2021, by section 13(2) of the New Zealand Superannuation and Retirement Income (Fair Residency) Amendment Act 2021 (2021 No 46).

part.heading: Provisions relating to Accident Compensation Amendment Act 2019

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 3: inserted, on 1 July 2019, by section 15 of the Accident Compensation Amendment Act 2019 (2019 No 10).

prov.heading: Certain veterans may elect rules of former scheme: weekly income compensation

para.text: In this clause, former provisions means subsections (6) to (11) of section 65 of this Act as they read immediately before the commencement of this clause.
This clause applies to a veteran who first became entitled to weekly income compensation—
under the former provisions; and
while the veteran was 64 years of age or older but not yet 65 years of age.

The veteran may make an election under subsection (8) of the former provisions in accordance with subsection (10) of the former provisions, and, if the veteran does so, the former provisions continue to apply to the veteran.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 18: inserted, on 1 July 2019, by section 15 of the Accident Compensation Amendment Act 2019 (2019 No 10).

prov.heading: Certain veterans may elect rules of former scheme: weekly compensation

para.text: In this clause, former provisions means subsections (6) to (11) of section 104 of this Act as they read immediately before the commencement of this clause.
This clause applies to a veteran who first became entitled to weekly compensation—
under the former provisions; and
while the veteran was 64 years of age or older but not yet 65 years of age.

The veteran may make an election under subsection (8) of the former provisions in accordance with subsection (10) of the former provisions, and, if the veteran does so, the former provisions continue to apply to the veteran.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 19: inserted, on 1 July 2019, by section 15 of the Accident Compensation Amendment Act 2019 (2019 No 10).

prov.heading: Certain other dependants may elect rules of former scheme

para.text: In this clause, former provisions means clauses 60(6) to (9) and 61 of Schedule 2 as they read immediately before the commencement of this clause.
This clause applies to any other dependant who first became entitled to weekly compensation—
under the former provisions; and
while the other dependant was 64 years of age or older but not yet 65 years of age.

The other dependant may make an election under clause 60(8) of the former provisions in accordance with clause 61 of the former provisions, and, if the other dependant does so, the former provisions continue to apply to the other dependant.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 20: inserted, on 1 July 2019, by section 15 of the Accident Compensation Amendment Act 2019 (2019 No 10).

part.heading: Provision relating to Social Assistance Legislation (Budget 2019 Welfare Package) Amendment Act 2019

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 4: inserted, on 6 June 2019, by section 21 of the Social Assistance Legislation (Budget 2019 Welfare Package) Amendment Act 2019 (2019 No 23).

prov.heading: Exercise of powers after enactment and before 1 April 2020

para.text: This clause applies to a power conferred by section 265(1)﻿(29A) (as that section is to be inserted by the Social Assistance Legislation (Budget 2019 Welfare Package) Amendment Act 2019).
The power may be exercised after the enactment of that Act and before the commencement on 1 April 2020 of that section.
If this clause is to be, or has been, relied on to exercise a power,—
all other enactments relevant to the power’s exercise, and that have not yet commenced, must be treated as if they had commenced; and
a legal position that would be conferred or imposed by an enactment relevant to the power’s exercise, and that has not yet commenced, must be treated as if it has accrued or been imposed.
Anything that results from the exercise of the power may take effect only on or after 1 April 2020.
However, that limit does not apply if the exercise of the power is necessary or desirable—
to bring the power, and all other enactments relevant to the power’s exercise, into operation; or
in connection with bringing the power, and all other enactments relevant to the power’s exercise, into operation; or
to amend, revoke, or replace anything previously done in reliance on this clause.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 21: inserted, on 6 June 2019, by section 21 of the Social Assistance Legislation (Budget 2019 Welfare Package) Amendment Act 2019 (2019 No 23).

part.heading: Provision relating to Veterans’ Support Amendment Act 2020

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 5: inserted, on 1 October 2020, by section 32 of the Veterans’ Support Amendment Act 2020 (2020 No 54).

prov.heading: Transitional ITOs as tertiary education organisations

para.text: This Part applies during the transition period.
Despite the definition of tertiary education organisation in section 81(5), a transitional ITO must also be treated as a tertiary education organisation for the purposes of section 81(1)﻿(b).
In this clause, transitional ITO and transition period have the same meanings as in clause 42 of Schedule 1 of the Education and Training Act 2020.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 22: inserted, on 1 October 2020, by section 32 of the Veterans’ Support Amendment Act 2020 (2020 No 54).

part.heading: Provision relating to New Zealand Superannuation and Veteran’s Pension Legislation Amendment Act 2020

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 6: inserted, on 9 November 2020, by section 34 of the New Zealand Superannuation and Veteran’s Pension Legislation Amendment Act 2020 (2020 No 36).

prov.heading: Generally abolished rate for veterans with non-qualifying spouse or partner

para.text: The relationship (partner not receiving superannuation or pension) rate is the appropriate rate of veteran’s pension payable to a person only if—
the person is a veteran; and
the person is married or in a civil union or in a de facto relationship; and
the person’s spouse or partner is not entitled to New Zealand superannuation or a veteran’s pension; and

immediately before 9 November 2020, an election of the person to receive a veteran’s pension, or New Zealand superannuation, at a non-standard couple rate was in effect (see clauses 8 to 10 of Schedule 1AA of the New Zealand Superannuation and Retirement Income Act 2001); and

the person is not barred under clause 7 of Schedule 1AA of the New Zealand Superannuation and Retirement Income Act 2001 from receiving New Zealand superannuation at the rate specified in clause 1(b) of Part 2 of Schedule 1 of that Act.
In this clause,—
non-standard couple rate has the same meaning as in clause 7(3) of Schedule 1AA of the New Zealand Superannuation and Retirement Income Act 2001
relationship (partner not receiving superannuation or pension) rate means the rate of that name referred to in section 169.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 23: inserted, on 9 November 2020, by section 34 of the New Zealand Superannuation and Veteran’s Pension Legislation Amendment Act 2020 (2020 No 36).

prov.heading: Application

para.text: Part 1 applies in relation to Scheme One and Scheme Two.
Parts 2 to 4 apply only in relation to Scheme Two.

prov.heading: Preparation of individual rehabilitation plan

para.text: VANZ must provide information to a veteran about—
the rehabilitation to which the veteran may be entitled; and
the plan development process; and

in relation to a plan that will include vocational rehabilitation, VANZ's right to require the veteran to undergo an assessment of his or her vocational independence at the completion of the vocational rehabilitation, and the potential consequences of such an assessment; and
the consequences of the veteran's agreeing to the plan.
VANZ may engage a suitably qualified person or organisation to—
assist in the assessment and preparation and costing of a plan; and
provide a link between the veteran and the services identified in the plan.
The following persons must be given an opportunity to participate in the preparation and costing of the plan to the extent that they are willing and able to do so:
the veteran:
any medical practitioner providing treatment to the veteran:
any employer or potential employer of the veteran.
VANZ is responsible for meeting the costs of preparing the plan, including the costs of any assessment that it has approved as necessary to enable the preparation of a plan.
In this clause, services includes the key aspects of social rehabilitation, as defined in section 121.

prov.heading: Agreement to plan

para.text: VANZ must then ask the veteran to agree to the plan prepared for the veteran under clause 2.
If, after a reasonable time, the veteran declines to agree to the plan, VANZ may advise the veteran that the plan is finalised, and the plan is then to be regarded as if the veteran had agreed to it.
When the plan is agreed or finalised, VANZ must implement the plan.
VANZ is responsible for funding the provision to the veteran of the services it has specified under section 118(2)﻿(d).

prov.heading: Disputes about plan

para.text: For the purposes of Part 7 of this Act, VANZ makes a decision when—
the veteran agrees to a plan; or
VANZ advises the veteran that a plan has been finalised.
The fact that a veteran has agreed to a plan does not affect his or her rights to make a review application under Part 7 of this Act with respect to the plan.

prov.heading: VANZ and veteran may agree to modify plan

para.text: The veteran and VANZ may agree to modify the plan from time to time, and clauses 2 to 4 apply to the process of modification and to the modified plan.

prov.heading: When VANZ responsible for paying or contributing to cost of ancillary services related to rehabilitation

para.text: VANZ is responsible for paying or contributing to the cost of any service reasonably required by the veteran as an ancillary service related to rehabilitation, such as accommodation, escort for transport, and transport, if the service facilitates rehabilitation.

VANZ is responsible for paying or contributing to the cost of accommodation and transport of a person other than the veteran if the presence and support of the person is necessary and appropriate to assist in achieving a rehabilitation outcome for the veteran.
This clause applies subject to any regulations made under this Act.

prov.heading: Definitions

para.text: In clauses 8 to 17,—
aid or appliance means any item likely to assist in restoring a veteran to independence
attendant care—
means—
personal care; and
assistance with cognitive tasks of daily living, such as communication, orientation, planning, and task completion; and
protection of the veteran from further injury or illness in his or her ordinary environment; and
includes training a person to provide attendant care, if VANZ agrees to fund the training; but
does not include child care, domestic activities, or home maintenance
child—
means a child under 14 years; and
includes any other child, if the child needs child care because of his or her physical or mental condition

communication means conveying and receiving information by using skills such as anger management, assertiveness, ability to concentrate, language, memory, numeracy, social awareness, social skills, speech production and development, and using communications technology
domestic activities means cleaning, laundry, meal preparation, and associated shopping activities, in relation to the veteran's home
home help means the provision of domestic activities
independence includes the capacity to function in the following areas:
communication:
domestic activities:
educational participation:
financial management:
health care:
hygiene care:
mobility:
motivation:
safety management:
sexuality:
cognitive tasks of daily living, such as orientation, planning, and task completion:
use of transport
modifications to the home—
means alterations to a veteran's home that—
have the purpose of assisting a veteran to live as independently as practicable, having regard to the limitations caused by his or her injury or illness; and
remove structural barriers or add features fixed to the home; and

includes real estate fees, legal fees, removal costs, and the costs of any modifications incurred in relocating a veteran to a new home, if VANZ decides that relocation is the most cost-effective alternative to modification of the veteran's existing home
personal care means physical assistance to move around and to take care of basic personal needs such as bathing, dressing, feeding, and toileting
rehabilitation outcome means,—
before an individual rehabilitation plan is agreed, a rehabilitation goal, objective, or result determined by VANZ:
if an individual rehabilitation plan is agreed, a goal, objective, or result specified in the plan
training for independence includes—
training and coaching a veteran to assist in restoring the veteran's independence:
training in using and maintaining aids or appliances, and integrating them into the veteran's daily life, for a veteran who needs aids or appliances
transport for independence—
means assistance with the cost of, for example,—
escorted travel by vehicle:
modifying a vehicle:
purchasing a vehicle:
travelling by public transport:
travelling by taxi; and
includes driver licence retraining, for a veteran who previously had a driver licence.

history.history-note: Schedule 2 clause 7 home: repealed, on 1 October 2020, by section 33(1) of the Veterans’ Support Amendment Act 2020 (2020 No 54).

prov.heading: Aids and appliances

para.text: In deciding whether to provide or contribute to the cost of an aid or appliance, VANZ must have regard to—
any rehabilitation outcome that would be achieved by providing it; and
whether a veteran has a prescription for the aid or appliance from a heath practitioner who holds appropriate qualifications to the satisfaction of VANZ.
VANZ is not required to provide an artificial aid in the nature of an implant, unless the implant is implanted in the course of a surgical procedure approved by VANZ.
VANZ is not required to provide any aid or appliance, if a veteran already—
owns an aid or appliance that has, at the time at which VANZ is making its decision, a similar function to the aid or appliance for which the veteran has made a claim; or
possesses such an aid or appliance on permanent loan from any person or organisation, including a hospital and health service,—
unless, in either case, the aid or appliance, because of its age or condition, is unsuitable to assist in restoring the veteran to independence.
VANZ is not required to provide any aid or appliance, if the veteran has, after suffering the injury or illness, disposed of an aid or appliance that, at the time of disposal,—
had a similar function to the aid or appliance for which the veteran has made a claim; and
was still suitable for that function.
VANZ is not required to meet any costs of—
maintaining, repairing, or replacing any aid or appliance; or
replacing any consumable items used in association with any aid or appliance,—
if the costs have been incurred because the veteran has neglected, abused, or misused the aid or appliance.
VANZ may provide an aid or appliance by way of approving its hire by the veteran for a term it approves, if that hire is a cost-effective alternative to the purchase of the aid or appliance.

prov.heading: Attendant care

para.text: In deciding whether to provide or contribute to the cost of attendant care, VANZ must have regard to—
any rehabilitation outcome that would be achieved by providing it; and
the nature and extent of the veteran's injury or illness and the degree to which that injury or illness impairs his or her ability to provide for his or her personal care; and

the extent to which attendant care is necessary to enable the veteran to undertake or continue employment (including agreed vocational training) or to attend a place of education, having regard to any entitlement the veteran has to education support; and
the extent to which household family members or other family members might reasonably be expected to provide attendant care for the veteran after the veteran's injury or illness; and
the extent to which attendant care is required to give household family members a break, from time to time, from providing attendant care for the veteran; and
the need to avoid substantial disruption to the employment or other activities of household family members.

prov.heading: Child care

para.text: In deciding whether to provide or contribute to the cost of child care, VANZ must have regard to—
any rehabilitation outcome that would be achieved by providing it; and
the number of the veteran's children and their need for child care; and
the extent to which child care was provided by other household family members before the veteran's injury or illness; and
the extent to which other household family members or other family members might reasonably be expected to provide child care services after the veteran's injury or illness; and
the need to avoid substantial disruption to the employment or other activities of the household family members.
VANZ is not required to provide child care under this clause if it provides child care for the child under clause 64.
VANZ is not required to provide child care for a child to the extent that the child is being provided with attendant care, education support, or training for independence.
VANZ is not required to pay for child care to the extent that child care continues to be provided after a veteran's injury or illness by a person—
who lives in the veteran's home or lived in the veteran's home immediately before the veteran suffered his or her injury or illness; and
who provided child care before the veteran suffered his or her injury or illness.

prov.heading: Home help

para.text: In deciding whether to provide or contribute to the cost of home help, VANZ must have regard to—
any rehabilitation outcome that would be achieved by providing it; and

the extent to which a veteran undertook domestic activities before the veteran's injury or illness and the extent to which he or she is able to undertake domestic activities after his or her injury or illness; and
the number of household family members and their need for home help; and
the extent to which domestic activities were done by other household family members before the veteran's injury or illness; and

the extent to which other household family members or other family members might reasonably be expected to do domestic activities for themselves and for the veteran after the veteran's injury or illness; and
the need to avoid substantial disruption to the employment or other activities of the household family members; and
the impact of the veteran's injury or illness on the contribution of other family members to domestic activities.
VANZ is not required to pay for home help to the extent that home help continues to be provided after a veteran's injury or illness by a person—
who lives in the veteran's home or lived in the veteran's home immediately before the veteran suffered his or her injury or illness; and
who provided home help before the veteran suffered his or her injury or illness.

prov.heading: Modifications to home: matters to which VANZ must have regard

para.text: In deciding whether to provide or contribute to the cost of modifications to the home, VANZ must have regard to—
any rehabilitation outcome that would be achieved by providing them; and
the difficulties faced by the veteran in doing the following without the proposed modifications:
gaining access to his or her home:
enjoying reasonable freedom of movement in his or her home:
living independently in his or her home; and
the likely duration of the veteran's residence in the home; and
the cost, and the relevant benefit, to the veteran of the proposed modifications; and
if the home is not owned by the veteran, whether the owner agrees to the modifications being done; and
the likely cost of reasonable alternative living arrangements; and
the likely duration of the limitations arising from the injury or illness for which the veteran has entitlements under this Act.
For the purposes of subclause (1)﻿(b)﻿(iii), the assessment of whether a veteran is living independently is not affected by whether the veteran lives with others.

prov.heading: Modifications to home: rights and responsibilities

para.text: VANZ is responsible for—
making a preliminary assessment as to whether the proposed modifications serve the purpose in section 120; and
if it considers the proposed modifications serve that purpose, meeting the costs of obtaining local authority approval.
The veteran is responsible for—
obtaining the written consent to the modifications to which VANZ has given preliminary approval from the owner of the home and any lessor or co-tenant or mortgagee; and
obtaining any quotes required by VANZ for the proposed modifications.
VANZ is—
not required to be a contracting party at any stage of the modifications:
not liable to the veteran or any other person for any liability arising from the carrying out of the modifications, other than for payment for those modifications VANZ has approved:
not liable—
to ensure that the veteran pays any person that undertakes the modifications; or
to pay that person directly, if the veteran does not pay:
not responsible for the cost of insuring the modifications or the home in which the modifications have been installed:
not required to replace any such modifications if the veteran continues to reside in the home:
not responsible for the cost of removing any modifications no longer required:
not responsible for the cost of returning a home to its former state if the veteran no longer occupies it:
not responsible for any loss of value of any home resulting from any modifications to, or removal of modifications from, the home:
not required to provide or contribute to the cost of modifications to a home to which the veteran moves from the modified home, unless VANZ has approved the costs of those modifications:
not required to repair or replace any home or modifications that are not insured and that are damaged.
VANZ is not entitled to recover any payment made to the veteran if—
the modifications are removed or are no longer required; or
the veteran no longer occupies the home to which the modifications were made.

prov.heading: Training for independence

para.text: In deciding whether to provide or contribute to the cost of training for independence, VANZ must have regard to—
any rehabilitation outcome that would be achieved by providing it; and
the extent to which training for independence, and the skills and knowledge likely to be acquired from it, are likely to reduce the veteran's need for further rehabilitation.

prov.heading: Transport for independence: matters to which VANZ must have regard

para.text: In deciding whether to provide or contribute to the cost of transport for independence, VANZ must have regard to—
any rehabilitation outcome that would be achieved by providing it; and
the cost, and the relevant benefit, to the veteran of the transport for independence service for which the veteran has made a claim; and

the difficulties faced by the veteran in doing the following in relation to the transport used by the veteran before his or her injury or illness, without the transport for independence service for which he or she has made a claim:
driving or operating the vehicle:
gaining access to the vehicle:
enjoying freedom and safety of movement in the vehicle:
travelling as a passenger in the vehicle:
transporting any essential mobility equipment in the vehicle; and
the need for the veteran to own or have access to a vehicle, having regard to the times at which and the frequency with which the veteran is likely to need that form of transport; and
alternative means of transport available to the veteran; and
the effect that modifications, or purchase, of a vehicle will have on the likelihood of the veteran obtaining and retaining employment; and
the existing vehicle or vehicles owned or used by the veteran; and
whether and when the limitations caused by the veteran's injury or illness are expected to improve; and
any plans and quotes an appropriately qualified person provides for proposed modifications to, or for the purchase of, a vehicle.
VANZ may require the veteran to satisfy VANZ of the matters set out in subclause (3) or (4) before VANZ approves the modification or purchase for which the veteran has made a claim.
VANZ may require the veteran to satisfy VANZ that a vehicle will be modified in such a way that it—
will be able to be issued with a warrant of fitness; and
will comply with or be exempted from any applicable rules made under Part 11 of the Land Transport Act 1998.

If a veteran wishes to drive a modified or newly purchased vehicle, VANZ may require the veteran to satisfy VANZ that he or she is likely to be able to drive the vehicle safely and be legally permitted to drive it.

prov.heading: Transport for independence: rights and responsibilities

para.text: VANZ is not required to purchase, or contribute to the purchase of, a motor vehicle if the veteran, or a person proposing to transport the veteran,—
owns or part owns or has the use of a vehicle that may be modified in order to assist in restoring the veteran's independence; or
disposed of a vehicle, after the veteran's injury or illness, that may have been able to be modified in order to assist in restoring the veteran's independence.
VANZ is—
not responsible—
for ensuring that the veteran pays any person that sells the vehicle or undertakes the modifications; or
for paying that person directly, if the veteran does not pay:
not required to meet the cost of maintenance or repair of any vehicle or modification to a vehicle, or for registration, licensing fees, insurance, or other running costs:
not required to meet the cost of removing any vehicle modifications no longer required or for restoring any vehicle to its former state:
not responsible for any loss of resale value resulting from modifications to any vehicle:

not required to contribute to the cost of replacing a vehicle for whose purchase or modification VANZ has already contributed, unless the replacement is necessary for the veteran to maintain independence:
not required to contribute to a replacement under paragraph (e) if the veteran's need for a replacement vehicle arises because the veteran—
has not maintained or insured the existing vehicle; or
has, without a reasonable excuse, disposed of the existing vehicle.

In determining the amount to be paid in respect of a vehicle, VANZ may take into account the value of any other motor vehicle owned by the veteran, if the veteran previously used the vehicle on a regular basis.
VANZ must—
make payments for the purchase of, or modification to, a vehicle to the veteran, unless the veteran requests otherwise; and
make the payment by way of an outright grant.
VANZ is not entitled to recover any payment made to the veteran if—
the veteran no longer requires the vehicle modifications; or
the vehicle is disposed of or destroyed.

prov.heading: VANZ not responsible for ensuring veteran pays rehabilitation provider

para.text: VANZ is not responsible—
for ensuring that the veteran pays any rehabilitation provider that delivers a social rehabilitation service to the veteran; or
for paying that provider directly, if the veteran does not pay.
To avoid doubt, subclause (1) does not prevent VANZ (if it thinks it appropriate to do so) from paying a provider directly, if a veteran does not pay.

prov.heading: Occupational assessor

para.text: An occupational assessment must be undertaken by an assessor whom VANZ considers has the appropriate qualifications and experience to do the assessment required in the particular case.

prov.heading: Conduct of occupational assessment

para.text: An occupational assessor undertaking an occupational assessment as part of an assessment of a veteran's vocational independence under section 130 may—
take into account information provided by VANZ and the veteran; and
consider the individual rehabilitation plan prepared for the veteran and review the vocational rehabilitation carried out under the plan; and
discuss with the veteran all the types of work that the assessor identifies as suitable for the veteran; and
consider any comments the veteran makes to the assessor about those types of work.

In considering the suitability of the types of work referred to in subclause (1)﻿(c), the occupational assessor may take into account, among other things, the veteran’s earnings before the veteran’s incapacity.
VANZ must provide to an occupational assessor all information VANZ has that is relevant to an occupational assessment.

prov.heading: Report on occupational assessment

para.text: The occupational assessor must prepare and provide to VANZ a report on the occupational assessment specifying—
the veteran's work experience; and
the veteran's education, including any incomplete formal qualifications; and
any work-related training in which the veteran has participated; and
all skills that the assessor has reasonably identified the veteran as having; and
the vocational rehabilitation that the veteran has received under the individual rehabilitation plan or in any other way; and
the outcome of the vocational rehabilitation; and
all types of work reasonably identified as suitable for the veteran; and

in relation to each type of work, the requirements of that type of work, including any environmental modifications that the assessor identifies as necessary to enable the veteran to function safely in that type of work.
VANZ must provide a copy of the report to the veteran and the medical assessor.

prov.heading: Conduct of medical assessment

para.text: A medical assessor undertaking a medical assessment as part of an assessment of a veteran's vocational independence under section 130 must take into account—
information provided to the assessor by VANZ; and
any individual rehabilitation plan for the veteran; and
any of the following medical reports provided to the assessor:
medical reports requested by VANZ before the individual rehabilitation plan was prepared:
medical reports received during the veteran's rehabilitation; and
the report of the occupational assessor under clause 20; and
the medical assessor's clinical examination of the veteran; and
any other information or comments that the veteran requests the medical assessor to take into account and that the medical assessor decides are relevant.
VANZ must provide to a medical assessor all information VANZ has that is relevant to a medical assessment.

prov.heading: Report on medical assessment

para.text: The medical assessor must prepare and provide to VANZ a report on the medical assessment specifying—
relevant details about the veteran, including details of the veteran's injury or illness; and
relevant details about the clinical examination of the veteran undertaken by the assessor, including the methods used and the assessor's findings from the examination; and
the results of any additional assessments of the veteran's condition; and
the assessor's opinion of the veteran's vocational independence in relation to each of the types of work identified in the occupational assessor's report; and

any comments made by the veteran to the assessor relating to the veteran's injury or illness and vocational independence in relation to each of the types of work identified in the occupational assessor's report.
The report must also identify any conditions that—
prevent the veteran from having vocational independence; and
are not related to the veteran's injury or illness.
VANZ must provide a copy of the report to the veteran.

part.heading: Weekly compensation (Scheme Two)

prov.heading: Meaning of relevant year

para.text: In this Part, relevant year means the most recent tax year (as defined in section YA 1 of the Income Tax Act 2007) last ended before the commencement of the period of incapacity.

However, in the case of a self-employed person or a shareholder-employee, the relevant year is the most recent year ending with the balance date (whether 31 March or another date) of the self-employed person or shareholder-employee before the commencement of the period of incapacity.

prov.heading: Use of income tax returns in determining earnings

para.text: If VANZ is determining earnings under this Part in relation to a self-employed person or a shareholder-employee, it must take an income tax return into account, if—
the veteran has given the return to the Commissioner; and
VANZ considers that the return, and any related accounts, have not been unreasonably influenced by—
the fact of the veteran's incapacity; or
the effects or likely effects of the incapacity on the veteran's income or business activities.

prov.heading: Minimum weekly compensation

para.text: Despite the other provisions of this Part, the weekly compensation payable under this Part must not be less than 80% of the average wage.
In this clause, average wage means the before tax average ordinary time weekly wage (all sectors, male and female combined) as at 1 July each year, as specified in the latest QES.

history.history-note: Schedule 2 clause 25(2): replaced, on 1 September 2022, by section 107(1) of the Data and Statistics Act 2022 (2022 No 39).

part.crosshead: VANZ to pay weekly compensation from first day of incapacity

prov.heading: VANZ to pay weekly compensation for loss of earnings to veteran who was earner

para.text: VANZ is responsible for paying weekly compensation for loss of earnings to a veteran who—
has an incapacity resulting from an injury or illness for which he or she has entitlements under this Act; and
was an earner immediately before his or her incapacity commenced.
The veteran is entitled to weekly compensation for loss of earnings for the period of the incapacity.
The weekly compensation payable is the specified percentage of the veteran's weekly earnings, as calculated under clauses 27 to 39 and 43.
In subclause (3), specified percentage means—
100% during the shorter of—
the first year of incapacity; or
the period during that year in which the veteran is receiving rehabilitation services:
where incapacity continues after the first year, 85% if either—
the veteran has a rehabilitation plan and is assessed as unable to work; or
the veteran is assessed as unable to be rehabilitated or further rehabilitated (as the case may be) and as unable to work.
Subclause (3) is subject to clauses 40, 43, and 44.
The amount of weekly compensation payable to the veteran must be adjusted in the manner provided in section 30.

prov.heading: Weekly earnings if earner had earnings as employee immediately before incapacity commenced: application of clause 28

para.text: Clause 28 applies to a veteran who—
was an earner immediately before his or her incapacity commenced; and
was in permanent employment at that time; and
had earnings as an employee from that permanent employment at that time.

If the veteran had permanent employment with more than 1 employer at that time, the weekly earnings of the veteran, in respect of each employer he or she had at that time, are as calculated separately under clause 28 and aggregated under clause 36.

For the purposes of this clause and clause 28, the veteran is regarded as having been in permanent employment if, in the opinion of VANZ, he or she would have continued to receive earnings from that employment for a continuous period of more than 12 months after the date on which his or her incapacity commenced, if he or she had not suffered the injury or illness.
Subclause (5) applies if—
the veteran was in permanent employment (that was full-time employment) as an employee immediately before his or her incapacity commenced; and
before the employment, the veteran was employed by the same employer for less than 30 hours per week.
The weekly earnings of the veteran is the greater of—
the veteran's weekly earnings calculated in accordance with clause 28; and
the veteran's weekly earnings calculated in accordance with clause 30, as if the veteran were not in permanent employment immediately before his or her incapacity commenced.

prov.heading: Weekly earnings if earner had earnings as employee in permanent employment immediately before incapacity commenced: calculations

para.text: This subclause applies to each of the 4 weeks after the first week of incapacity. The veteran's weekly earnings for each of the 4 weeks are calculated using the following formula:
where—
is the veteran's earnings as an employee (from that permanent employment) in the 4 weeks immediately before his or her incapacity commenced
is the number of full or part weeks during which the veteran earned those earnings as an employee in those 4 weeks.

This subclause applies to any weekly period of incapacity after the 4 weeks described in subclause (1). The veteran's weekly earnings for any such weekly period are calculated using the following formula:
where—
is the veteran's earnings as an employee (from employment with that employer) in the 52 weeks immediately before his or her incapacity commenced
is the number of full or part weeks during which the veteran earned those earnings as an employee.
For the purposes of this clause, the following must be disregarded in calculating weekly earnings:
any period during which the veteran was entitled to weekly compensation; and
any earnings in respect of any such period.

prov.heading: Weekly earnings if earner had earnings as employee not in permanent employment immediately before incapacity commenced: application of clause 30

para.text: Clause 30 applies to a veteran who—
was an earner immediately before his or her incapacity commenced; and
had at that time earnings as an employee (from employment that was not permanent employment).

For the purposes of this clause and clause 30, employment is not permanent employment if, in the opinion of VANZ, the veteran would have not continued to receive earnings from that employment for a continuous period of more than 12 months after the date on which his or her incapacity commenced, if he or she had not suffered the injury or illness.

prov.heading: Weekly earnings if earner had earnings as employee not in permanent employment immediately before incapacity commenced: calculations

para.text: This subclause applies to each of the 4 weeks after the first week of incapacity. The veteran's weekly earnings for each of the 4 weeks are calculated using the following formula:
where—
is the veteran's earnings as an employee (from all employment that was not permanent employment) in the 4 weeks immediately before his or her incapacity commenced
is the number of full or part weeks during which the veteran earned those earnings as an employee in the 4 weeks immediately before his or her incapacity commenced.

This subclause applies to any weekly period of incapacity after the 4 weeks described in subclause (1). The veteran's weekly earnings for any such weekly period are calculated using the following formula:
where—
is the veteran's earnings as an employee (from all employment that was not permanent employment) in the 52 weeks immediately before his or her incapacity commenced
is 52 or such smaller number, if adjustments are required under subclause (4).
For the purposes of this clause, the following must be disregarded in calculating weekly earnings:
any period during which the veteran was entitled to weekly compensation:
any continuous period of unpaid sick leave, during a period of employment, of more than 1 week:
any period during which—
the veteran did not receive earnings as an employee; and
the veteran did receive earnings as a self-employed person or as a shareholder-employee; and
those earnings ceased before the commencement of the veteran's incapacity:
any earnings in respect of any period under paragraph (a), (b), or (c).
